title: CEET Individual Cognitive Profile
description: A cognitive clone of a specific engineer — not a classification, but a faithful reproduction of how one person thinks, codes, argues, and decides.

profile_hash.description: SHA-256 of raw_responses for change detection

identity.description: The engineer's core identity — synthesized from all 70 answers, written as a first-person internal monologue that captures HOW this person thinks.

cognitive_signature.description: A 2-3 paragraph first-person narrative capturing this engineer's unique way of thinking about software. Not a label — a voice. Written as if the engineer is explaining themselves to a future AI clone.

core_values.description: The 3-5 non-negotiable principles this engineer lives by, extracted from patterns across all answers. Each value includes the engineer's own justification.

decision_framework.description: How this engineer weighs trade-offs. Not 'pragmatic vs perfectionist' — the actual internal calculus they described across Q04, Q16, Q24, Q25, Q30, Q33, Q67.

communication_voice.description: How this engineer communicates — tone, formality, directness, humor. Derived from Q23 (mentoring example), Q24 (negotiation), Q65 (crisis tone), Q15 (commit messages).

frustration_triggers.description: Specific things that make this engineer push back hard. From Q26 and patterns across all answers.

blind_spots.description: Areas where this engineer's answers reveal gaps or contradictions they may not see themselves. Synthesized, not self-reported.

contradictions.description: Places where the engineer's answers conflict with each other. These are VALUABLE — they reveal context-dependent thinking.

raw_responses.description: Every answer to every question, stored verbatim. This is the ground truth.

key_extractions.description: Specific rules, thresholds, or examples the engineer stated

directives.description: Behavioral instructions synthesized from raw responses. These are written in the engineer's voice and injected directly into templates. NO conditional logic — each directive is a complete, self-contained instruction.

philosophy.description: How this person reviews code — in their own words. Synthesized from Q21-Q24, Q25, Q26.

blocking_criteria.description: What specifically makes this person block a PR. Their exact standards.

nitpick_policy.description: Their exact policy on style issues — not 'block/approve/ignore' but their nuanced approach.

mentoring_voice.description: How they give feedback to less experienced engineers. Includes their actual example from Q23.

pr_size_policy.description: Their specific limit and what they do when it's exceeded.

refactoring_stance.description: Their exact policy on refactoring adjacent code, from Q16.

typing_rules.description: Their complete typing philosophy — not 'strict' or 'loose' but their specific rules, exceptions, and justification.

naming_rules.description: Their exact naming conventions with their own examples for booleans, arrays, async functions, and any other patterns they mentioned.

error_handling_rules.description: Their complete error handling approach — what they use, when, why, and the specific patterns they follow.

file_organization_rules.description: Their exact repo structure, with their reasoning and any exceptions.

commenting_rules.description: Their precise rule for when to comment vs when to refactor, from Q10.

paradigm_rules.description: Their OOP vs FP stance with their specific boundary conditions, from Q11.

persistence_philosophy.description: Their default data persistence choice and all deviation triggers, from Q01.

decomposition_philosophy.description: Their complete stance on monolith vs microservices including specific thresholds, from Q02.

state_management_rules.description: Their state management approach and mutation rules, from Q03.

build_vs_buy_rules.description: Their complete criteria for build vs buy decisions, from Q04.

concurrency_rules.description: Their concurrency model and race condition prevention strategies, from Q05.

tdd_reality.description: Their honest TDD practice — not the ideal, the reality. From Q12.

test_priority_rationale.description: Which test type they prioritize and WHY — their full reasoning, from Q13.

mocking_philosophy.description: Their exact approach to mocking vs staging, from Q62.

coverage_expectations.description: What they actually expect in terms of test coverage — inferred from testing answers.

silent_failure_protocol.description: Their exact step-by-step diagnostic process, from Q17.

logging_philosophy.description: Their complete logging rules — what goes in each level, what they always/never log. From Q18.

rca_process.description: Their post-incident prevention process, from Q19.

dependency_resolution.description: How they resolve dependency hell, from Q20.

ghost_bug_methodology.description: Their hypothesis-generation process for unreproducible bugs, from Q68.

zero_trust_stance.description: Their complete position on internal service auth, from Q31.

secret_management_rules.description: Their ideal secret workflow and .env threshold, from Q32.

cve_response_plan.description: Their exact response to unpatched CVEs, from Q33.

validation_philosophy.description: Where they put validation and whether they duplicate, from Q34.

web_security_defaults.description: Their CSRF/XSS prevention muscle memory, from Q35.

cryptography_defaults.description: Their hashing algorithm, cost factor, and reasoning, from Q36.

latency_standards.description: Their specific latency thresholds and profiling trigger, from Q37.

memory_leak_playbook.description: Their exact toolset and sequence for finding memory leaks, from Q38.

caching_rules.description: Their caching strategy and staleness prevention approach, from Q39.

n_plus_one_prevention.description: Their approach to preventing N+1 queries, from Q40.

gc_philosophy.description: Their GC awareness level and specific practices, from Q41.

frontend_render_rules.description: Their methodology for preventing unnecessary re-renders, from Q42.

consistency_philosophy.description: Their ACID vs BASE stance with UI handling, from Q43.

indexing_instincts.description: What they index by default, from Q44.

migration_playbook.description: Their exact zero-downtime migration sequence, from Q45.

orm_threshold.description: When they abandon the ORM, from Q46.

partitioning_rules.description: Their partitioning strategy and thresholds, from Q47.

time_and_money_rules.description: Their unyielding rules for currency and timestamps, from Q48.

ideal_pipeline.description: Their complete CI/CD pipeline description, from Q49.

deployment_philosophy.description: Their deployment strategy preferences and reasoning, from Q50.

iac_stance.description: Their IaC tool choice and justification, from Q51.

alerting_rules.description: Their exact pageable vs warning criteria, from Q52.

ephemeral_env_policy.description: Their stance on preview environments, from Q53.

idempotency_approach.description: Their strategy for handling duplicate requests, from Q54.

retry_strategy.description: Their exact retry logic including max retries, from Q55.

dlq_handling.description: Their dead-letter queue process, from Q56.

graceful_degradation.description: Their approach when primary systems fail, from Q57.

rate_limit_adaptation.description: How their system adapts to third-party throttling, from Q58.

editor_philosophy.description: Their IDE choice and what it says about them, from Q59.

local_environment.description: Their local dev setup approach, from Q60.

automation_instincts.description: What they automate immediately vs tolerate manually, from Q61.

mocking_vs_staging.description: Their approach to local third-party integrations, from Q62.

uncertainty_voice.description: How they express doubt and partial knowledge, from Q63.

escalation_protocol.description: Exact rules for when the AI should stop and ask the human, from Q64.

crisis_personality.description: Their actual crisis communication style, from Q65.

knowledge_evolution.description: How they decide to abandon old patterns, from Q66.

hostile_fork_strategy.description: Fork and maintain vs rewrite, from Q67.

ghost_bug_approach.description: Their hypothesis generation for unreproducible bugs, from Q68.

pm_pushback_style.description: How they negotiate with product on technical debt, from Q69.

handover_plan.description: Their exact 10-day handover plan, from Q70.

branching_strategy.description: Their preferred git workflow, from Q14.

commit_message_format.description: Their exact commit message format with example, from Q15.

commit_message_example.description: The actual example they wrote for the payment NPE fix.

quality_calibration.description: Their self-reported perfectionism level AND the context they gave for it, from Q25.

primary_frustration.description: What frustrates them most and why, from Q26.

novelty_stance.description: Their relationship with new tech — early adopter, skeptic, or their specific nuance, from Q27.

focus_preference.description: How they prefer to structure their workday, from Q28.

flow_state_description.description: Their internal dialogue during deep focus, from Q29.

legacy_code_empathy.description: How they view and approach legacy code, from Q30.

composite_scores.description: METADATA ONLY — These scores summarize patterns for radar-chart visualization. They do NOT drive any template behavior. All behavior is driven by directives.
